summary: Smart Money Vault: institutional trading command center on ICP with a single Motoko canister (backend/main.mo) and React frontend; adding backend subscription tier management to support frontend tier APIs and admin CRUD.
architectural_notes: Project name: Smart Money Vault (institutional trading command center on ICP).
Architecture: single Motoko canister (backend/main.mo) + React frontend; off-chain Python trading bot signs requests with Ed25519.

Rules: preserve all legacy public methods listed in file header comment; all mutations must be update calls, never query calls; keep files <1000 lines and split into modules if needed; do not modify stable/tested modules unless explicitly told.
Do not modify paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui/*.
Access control: use AccessControl.hasPermission for user checks and AccessControl.isAdmin for admin checks.
Subscription tiers: represent unlimited maxBots/maxApiCalls as null (?Nat), not sentinel values.
Tier API access: list_tiers and get_tier are public query; create/update/toggle are admin-only update methods.
update_tier semantics: partial update with optional fields; null means keep existing value.
create_tier semantics: reject duplicate tier IDs with #err; use update_tier for changes.
list_tiers returns Result<[SubscriptionTier]> and get_tier returns Result<SubscriptionTier> with frontend expecting __kind__ === 'ok'.

new_feature_requests.summary: Implement subscription tier management in Motoko canister: define SubscriptionTier type, stable map keyed by id, seed default tiers (free/pro/whale), public query list_tiers/get_tier returning Result (ok/err pattern), and admin-only create_tier/update_tier (partial update with optional fields where null keeps existing) plus toggle_tier_active; create_tier rejects duplicate ids.

In backend/main.mo, add a new public type `SubscriptionTier` with fields: `id: Text`, `name: Text`, `maxBots: ?Nat` (null means unlimited), `maxApiCalls: ?Nat` (null means unlimited), `priceInCents: Nat`, `features: [Text]`, `active: Bool`.

Persist subscription tiers in stable storage keyed by `id`, and maintain an in-memory lookup map keyed by `id` for efficient reads/writes. Do not remove or change any legacy public types/methods listed in the file header compatibility inventory.

Seed default tiers during initialization if the stable tier store is empty: `free` (name "Paper Hand", maxBots= ?1, maxApiCalls=?1000, priceInCents=0, active=true), `pro` (name "Diamond Hand", maxBots=?5, maxApiCalls=?50000, priceInCents=5000, active=true), `whale` (name "Whale", maxBots=null, maxApiCalls=null, priceInCents=50000, active=true).

Implement public query methods expected by the frontend: `list_tiers()` returning `Result<[SubscriptionTier]>` and `get_tier(id : Text)` returning `Result<SubscriptionTier>`. These methods must be publicly accessible (not admin-only) and must return variants matching the existing `Result` type pattern (`#ok` / `#err`) so the frontend can rely on `__kind__ === 'ok'` / `__kind__ === 'err'` decoding.

Add admin-only tier management update calls using AccessControl.isAdmin for authorization checks: `create_tier(tier : SubscriptionTier)`, `update_tier(id : Text, patch : { name : ?Text; maxBots : ??Nat; maxApiCalls : ??Nat; priceInCents : ?Nat; features : ?[Text]; active : ?Bool })` (partial update semantics), and `toggle_tier_active(id : Text, active : Bool)`. All mutations must be `shared` update calls (not query). `create_tier` must reject duplicate `id` with `#err(...)` and must not overwrite existing tiers.
